A, title XII, § 1206139 Stat. 1082Dec. 18, 2025“The Secretary of Defense shall make such changes to Department of Defense Instruction 5111.20 (relating to the State Partnership Program), or a successor instruction, as may be necessary to ensure that, in performing selection analysis for the State Partnership Program under , United States Code, the Chief of the National Guard Bureau considers—section 341 of title 10“(1)the number of current partnerships assigned to the National Guard of a State; and“(2)the capacity of the National Guard of a State in relation to the number of assigned countries.”

State Partnership Program Selection Analysis, , , provided that:Pub. L. 119–60, div. A, title XII, § 1206139 Stat. 1082Dec. 18, 2025“The Secretary of Defense shall make such changes to Department of Defense Instruction 5111.20 (relating to the State Partnership Program), or a successor instruction, as may be necessary to ensure that, in performing selection analysis for the State Partnership Program under , United States Code, the Chief of the National Guard Bureau considers—section 341 of title 10“(1)the number of current partnerships assigned to the National Guard of a State; and“(2)the capacity of the National Guard of a State in relation to the number of assigned countries.”

children.label: A
children.text: The military forces of a foreign country.

children.label: B
children.text: The security forces of a foreign country.

children.label: C
children.text: Governmental organizations of a foreign country whose primary functions include disaster response or emergency response.

children.heading: .—State partnership
children.label: 2 .—State partnership
children.text: Each program established under this subsection shall be known as a “State Partnership”.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: An activity with forces referred to in subsection (a)(1)(B) or organizations described in subsection (a)(1)(C) under a program established under subsection (a) may be carried out only if the Secretary of Defense, with the concurrence of the Secretary of State, determines and notifies the appropriate congressional committees not less than 15 days before initiating such activity that the activity is in the national security interests of the United States.

children.heading: .—Prohibition on activities with units that have committed gross violations of human rights
children.label: 2 .—Prohibition on activities with units that have committed gross violations of human rights
children.text: The conduct of any assistance activities under a program established under subsection (a) shall be subject to the provisions of .section 362 of this title

children.heading: .—Coordination of Activities
children.label: c .—Coordination of Activities
children.text: The Chief of the National Guard Bureau shall designate a director for each State and territory to be responsible for the coordination of activities under a program established under subsection (a) for such State or territory and reporting on activities under the program.

children.heading: .—Regulations
children.label: d .—Regulations
children.text: This section shall be carried out in accordance with such regulations as the Secretary of Defense shall prescribe for purposes of this section. Such regulations shall include accounting procedures to ensure that expenditures of funds to carry out this section are accounted for and appropriate.

children.heading: Availability of Authorized Funds for Program.—
children.label: e Availability of Authorized Funds for Program.—
A
children.text: for payment of costs incurred by the National Guard of a State or territory to conduct activities under a program established under subsection (a); and

children.label: B
children.text: for payment of incremental expenses of a foreign country to conduct activities under a program established under subsection (a).

children.heading: .—Active duty requirement
children.label: A .—Active duty requirement
children.text: Funds shall not be available under paragraph (1) for the participation of a member of the National Guard of a State or territory in activities in a foreign country unless the member is on active duty in the Armed Forces at the time of such participation.

children.heading: .—Incremental expenses
children.label: B .—Incremental expenses
children.text: The total amount of payments for incremental expenses of foreign countries as authorized under paragraph (1)(B) for activities under programs established under subsection (a) in any fiscal year may not exceed $10,000,000.

children.heading: .—Rule of Construction
children.label: f .—Rule of Construction
children.text: Nothing in this section shall be construed to supersede any authority under title 10 as in effect on .December 26, 2013
